# Quote

<TierBadge tier="value" />

Get the latest NBBO quote snapshot for an option contract.

- Retrieve a real-time last NBBO quote of an option contract.
- This endpoint will return no data if the market was closed for the day. Theta Data resets the snapshot cache at midnight ET every night.

<SdkTabs>

<template #rust>

```rust
pub fn option_snapshot_quote(&self, symbol: &str, expiration: &str) -> OptionSnapshotQuoteBuilder<'_>
```

Optional parameters chain on the builder: `.strike(&str)`, `.right(&str)`, `.max_dte(i32)`, `.strike_range(i32)`, `.min_time(&str)`. Execute with `.await` → `Result<Vec<QuoteTick>, Error>`, or decode chunk-by-chunk with `.stream(handler)`.

**Example**

```rust
let rows = client
    .historical()
    .option_snapshot_quote("SPY", "20250321")
    .strike("570")
    .right("C")
    .await?;
for t in &rows {
    println!("date={} ms_of_day={} bid={} ask={}", t.date, t.ms_of_day, t.bid, t.ask);
}
```

</template>

<template #python>

```python
Client.historical.option_snapshot_quote(
    symbol, expiration,
    *,
    strike=None, right=None, max_dte=None, strike_range=None, min_time=None,
    timeout_ms=None,
) -> list[QuoteTick]
```

`option_snapshot_quote_async(...)` awaits the same call shape.

**Example**

```python
rows = client.historical.option_snapshot_quote(
    "SPY",
    "20250321",
    strike="570",
    right="C",
)
for t in rows:
    print(t.date, t.ms_of_day, t.bid, t.ask)
```

</template>

<template #typescript>

```typescript
optionSnapshotQuote(
  symbol: string, expiration: string | Date, options?: { ... },
): Promise<Array<QuoteTick>>
```

Optional parameters ride in a single trailing options object: `strike?: string`, `right?: string`, `maxDTE?: number`, `strikeRange?: number`, `minTime?: string | Date`, `timeoutMs?: number`.

**Example**

```typescript
const rows = await client.historical.optionSnapshotQuote('SPY', '20250321', { strike: '570', right: 'C' });
for (const t of rows) {
  console.log(t.date, t.msOfDay, t.bid, t.ask);
}
```

</template>

<template #cpp>

```cpp
std::vector<QuoteTick> option_snapshot_quote(
    const std::string& symbol,
    const std::string& expiration,
    const EndpointRequestOptions& options = {}) const;
```

Optional parameters chain on `EndpointRequestOptions`: `.with_strike(...)`, `.with_right(...)`, `.with_max_dte(...)`, `.with_strike_range(...)`, `.with_min_time(...)`. Throws `thetadatadx::Error` on failure.

**Example**

```cpp
auto rows = client.historical().option_snapshot_quote("SPY", "20250321",
    thetadatadx::EndpointRequestOptions{}.with_strike("570").with_right("C"));
for (const auto& t : rows) {
    std::cout << t.date << ' ' << t.ms_of_day << ' ' << t.bid << ' ' << t.ask << "\n";
}
```

</template>

<template #http>

```http
GET http://127.0.0.1:25503/v3/option/snapshot/quote
```

Served by the bundled [server binary](/server/); responses stream as JSON, CSV, or NDJSON via the `format` parameter.

**Example**

```bash
curl -G 'http://127.0.0.1:25503/v3/option/snapshot/quote' \
    --data-urlencode 'symbol=SPY' \
    --data-urlencode 'expiration=2025-03-21' \
    --data-urlencode 'strike=570' \
    --data-urlencode 'right=C'
```

</template>

</SdkTabs>

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | yes | — | Ticker symbol (e.g. AAPL) |
| `expiration` | date | yes | — | Expiration date YYYYMMDD |
| `strike` | string | no | `*` | Strike price in dollars as a string (e.g. 500 or 17.5). Use `*` for wildcard selection. |
| `right` | string | no | `both` | Option side. Accepted values: `call`, `put`, `both`. |
| `max_dte` | int | no | — | Maximum days to expiration |
| `strike_range` | int | no | — | Strike range filter |
| `min_time` | string | no | — | Minimum time filter |
| `timeout_ms` | int | no | — | Per-request deadline in milliseconds. 0 means no deadline. |

## Response

Rows of `QuoteTick`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ms_of_day` | i32 | Milliseconds since midnight Eastern Time. |
| `bid_size` | i32 | Last NBBO bid size. |
| `bid_exchange` | i32 | Exchange code of the NBBO bid. |
| `bid` | f64 | Last NBBO bid price. |
| `bid_condition` | i32 | Quote condition code on the bid side. |
| `ask_size` | i32 | Last NBBO ask size. |
| `ask_exchange` | i32 | Exchange code of the NBBO ask. |
| `ask` | f64 | Last NBBO ask price. |
| `ask_condition` | i32 | Quote condition code on the ask side. |
| `date` | i32 | Trading date as a YYYYMMDD integer. |

Wildcard requests additionally populate `expiration` (YYYYMMDD), `strike` (dollars), and `right` ("C" / "P") on every row to identify the contract; on single-contract requests these are absent (None / null / undefined; the Rust and C rows carry the documented `0` / `0.0` / `'\0'` fills).
